Exploration Mission Report

Between April 5th and April 30th, Outpost 7, supported by Outpost 10, embarked on explorations and data retrieval missions to several remnant ruins belonging to the pre-war civilization. Despite being guarded by Mechanos forces, we visited 23 pre-war sites in total, which included 4 scientific research centers, 6 research companies, and 13 research-focused universities.
From these expeditions, Outpost 7 managed to bring back 46 research papers and 62 prototypes belonging to the research into X-Ionic III, alongside 23 papers and 34 prototypes related to railgun technologies. The biggest discovery was the designs for an Orbital Strike Cannon prototype.
Utilizing the gathered information, Commander Polina has finalized an improved design for the OSC MKII.
Questions have arisen concerning the impossible physical requirement to operate the Model II cannon, for which Commander Polina has already devised a plan to address.
Access to the detailed report and data requires authorization from Commander Polina.

To the board members of the Greg Industries,

I'll cut straight to the chase. Our single-pilot combat mech prototype, codename Ranger has exceeded all our performance expectations through its design, assembly, and preliminary tests.

We're poised for the final weapon system test, I do not need to remind you the significance of this momentous leap toward redefining future warfare and cementing Greg Industries in military manufacturing history. However, our current progress is impeded by three major pain points.
First of all, we urgently require experienced test pilots with real combat experience, and not just theoretical knowledge, to push our prototype to its absolute performance boundary.
Additionally, an immediate supply of high-end military-grade materials must be scheduled with absolute urgency. I want the absolute best, no-expense-spared material money can buy.
Lastly, to shift from prototype to mass production, an upgraded production line is essential for efficient manufacturing of each combat unit.

My focus is solely on transforming our designs into tangible realities for battlefield deployment, and not on petty bureaucracies or bickering politics.
This is a pivotal chance for Greg Industries to lead once again. Let's not be deterred by shortsighted distractions.
I look forward to decisive actions.

Julian

Research Notes

Location: Special Materials Laboratory
Weather: A storm is approaching, but our research must proceed without fail. 

Ever since the pre-war era, Xenium has been valued for its superior physicochemical properties, believing it to be a catalyst that will propel humanity in the futuristic beyond. This modified catalytic metal had almost endless military and industrial applications. However, its effects on the human body remained a mystery until recently.

Our experiment aimed to explore the effects of inhaling vaporized Xenium in the human body. Initial attempts to use highly diluted Xenium to enhance soldiers' muscle strength and reaction speed resulted in extreme instability in subject D-033, ultimately leading to cardiac arrest.

During an accident, we discovered an unknown new compound in the blood sample of the "Nebulas". This compound contained trace amounts of Xenium and exhibited astonishing fusion catalysis properties. I have named this new compound "X-Ionic III".

The discovery of X-Ionic III offered a glimmer of hope for the safe usage of Xenium on biological organisms. I need more time to validate my findings. I just hope that we are not opening a Pandora's Box.

Emily

Dear Uncle Mike,

As I write this, I'm seated inside a rescue transport, finding it hard to even keep my pen stable.
A moment ago, AETOS fired upon me. I felt as if I was an ant under the looming shadow of a giant's descending foot. That moment filled me with a fear I've never known before, a complete sense of helplessness with no place to flee or hide. I can hardly remember how I ended up on this transport.
I was convinced my end was near as my child days flashed before my eyes, how the world seemed so slow and mundane. I'm still trying to grasp with reality of losing everyone whom I used to call family, with you as my last living kin on this planet.
I find myself gazing upward every day, fearing the clear skies. The glowing ROS in the sky constantly reminds me that death is always just moments away.
I often ponder whether I would've lived my life differently had I known how precious those what I thought were mundane days truly were.
Our commander insists that we recite our oath to the EUA every day. Perhaps the only way to defeat fear is to constantly confront it every day.

Sincerely, Jason Rolle

(A discarded piece of paper with words are scattered without order, as if it was a memo that someone had scribbled down randomly.)

What I've believed in for so long now appears fragile against the harshness of reality, but I harbor no regrets.

I tried my best to shield our children against the turmoil. However, reality harshly reminds me that even the mightiest bastions fall in the face of hunger and fear. I've witnessed our once nurturing community turn into a desperate battlefield for survival.

Conflict. Sorrow. Defeat. Danger.

I hold onto the hope that there is still a sliver of light waiting for those who dare to search for it.

While holding a weapon, press open the Core Gun to infuse it with XEN to enhance its effect while firing.
When selecting the Artillery, press to select the bombardment location.

"Landmark" (red site) has high risks and better opportunities.
'Quick Recovery" will not draw in nearby enemies.
You can throw the detector to detect nearby items. Who knows what secrets you might find?
The Event Generator generates a random event that will impact your tour. Its power won't last long; you will need to power it up if you wish to generate events after its power runs out.
[Fateful Events] provide significant boosts to your combat capabilities but may also influence the likelihood of different types of events refreshing. Choose them carefully.
The [Assembler] allows you to utilize the materials in your personal backpack to instantly generate the required items on the battlefield.
Use [Left Mouse Click] to select and activate the turrets.

To interact with components, select and place them with LMB, rotate them using the mouse wheel, adjust the camera angle by holding the middle or right mouse button, and navigate the view with the WASD keys. Raise or lower the camera angle with Q and E.
Regular Recovery causes a lot of noise and attracts nearby enemies, while Quick Recovery doesn't.
The Titan Spike can expand the X-Boost Field, the Stakewall is a good defensive installment, and the MG Turret is your most reliable killing machine.
[Extreme Weather], including [Fog], [Rainstorm], [Blizzard], and [Sandstorm], negatively affects your Outpost and makes it easier for the Mechanos to detect you.

The Resource Transport Pod is the primary source for obtaining loot and enhancements in Dawn Base.
Different Pod models offer different items or events.
Pods refresh after a certain number of waves once opened.
Recovered Blueprints unlock new researches in the Research Center, such as new components for the Outpost.
Light Guns are effective against "huge" armored enemies.

Access to certain rooms is restricted and requires specific keycards, which can either be found nearby or during other Exploration Days. Murray typically take these keycards away without paying you much, so it's advisable to use them all while you're in the field.
Some explorations require a specified item that you have to recover before leaving the area.
Some POIs are guarded by enemies. Get ready for a fight or eliminate them ahead of time.
Some items/skills/equipment provides a shield that protects you from long range enemy fires.

This means that components on your Outpost are either colliding, unstable, or is placed outside of the boundaries. When this happens, you'll be unable to save your changes unless you remove the offending component.

Each different weapon caliber counters a specific type of enemy. Smaller calibers are countered by armored enemies, but larger caliber weapons deal more damage. You can use the [Adjustment Mode] to set each different turret's targeting priority.
Different XEN grant different effects to your weapons. XEN must be infused into a Core Gun to take effect.
Core Towers generate an X-Boost Field that provides power to personnel and equipment. Shields can only recharge while inside an X-Boost Field, and support facilities can only be placed within it.
The Core Tower provides power to all components. Some components (such as floors) come with pre-laid wiring that can bring power to components that cannot function otherwise.

The Core Tower provides power to all components. Some components (such as floors) come with pre-laid wirings that can bring power to components that cannot function otherwise. If a component does not receive sufficient power, it will not be able to function and you will not be able to receive any Connection Bonus.
Fully utilize your Outpost and Support Facilities to effectively combat large enemy forces. Don't be a lone wolf!
The culmination of human ingenuity. The primary method that Harvesters employ to avoid being obliterated by the AETOS.
You will not lose anything for aborting Main Objectives.
Rest Stations are temporary hideouts constructed by the EUA. You'll find supplies and relative safety inside.

In Casual and Rougelike Modes, you won't lose your Operative or Resources, but the rewards you receive will be limited.
In Survival Mode, you will use your own Operative and Outpost with death penalties consistent with those in Outpost Mode.
Looks like it can be used to generate power...
Seems to be some kind of power generator.
You can check the status of your Operatives here and adjust their loadout.
You can send drones to gather resources from within the X-Boost Field. This consumes more power but frees you from the job.
You can convert almost anything into Matter, which can be used to deploy additional structures and equipment.
You can submit items through the Core Tower and call it a day at any time.
You can spend [Insurance Points] for items of Common quality or above. If that item is lost when an Operative goes MIA, it will be retrieved and sent back to you.
You have completed the Exploration Day. Activate the Core Tower to extract with your Outpost.
You've completed a round of exploration. The resources that you've brought back are stored in the warehouse. You can use them to prepare the Outpost and operatives for more challenging tasks ahead.
You have obtained the blueprints for the [30mm Light Gun]. Corresponding research has bee unlocked.
Keep track of your ammunition. Replenish ammo through ammo crates and other means if it's running out.
You've got plenty of resources! You can craft some ammo.
Your main weapon may not work in certain areas. Proceed with caution.
Your mag size determines your damage output in a single magazine. You can't use a XEN if its formula costs more ammo than your mag size.
Your backpack is starting to fill up! You can reorganize your inventory by placing the items more strategically or depositing items into the field storage.
You have obtained a XEN. Hold [Q] to open the Core Gun menu and imbue it with XEN to modify your weapon.
You decided to hold your ground!
Press WASD to move; rotate camera with MMB.
Double-click the Cavalry Core Gun with [LMB] to equip it.
Resources recovered by drones will be sent back to the user's base.
Using any form of mobility assistance or XEN will let you reach places that you normally can't.
Shoot the ice wall with Pyro XEN.
Use a random Operative and preset Outpost to enjoy a fast-paced scavenge and battle.
Food replenishes your HP over time and increases your maximum HP.
The portable support facility is a temporary installment. You can equip it on your Outpost or Construction Vehicle. It will still be available on the next day.

Defend the [Core Tower] against a specified number of enemy attack waves.
If the [Core Tower] is destroyed or if your Operative dies, the challenge fails.
Use the preparation time effectively to fortify your defenses or gather additional resources.
Killing enemies within the X-Boost Field will generate resource.
Killing enemies grants resources. They're used to craft ammo and support facilities.
Destroy the enemy's signal beacon to skip this wave.

Manufacturing is a focus of the Research Center: here, you can manufacture components and consumables with items in the Base Warehouse and use them to further enhance your Outpost or facilitate your tours.
Let your Stakewalls and turrets handle them. Remember, you don't wanna get too close to them!
Use the interval between waves to scavenge near your base. Look for equipment and Fortunes to enhance your Operative and Outpost.
Outpost Value is the total value of all installed Outpost components. This can be viewed as a power indicator for your Outpost.
The Core Tower is under attack! Once it's destroyed, your squad and Outpost will be under the AETOS's threat and you will lose items in the container.
Scour each corner of the globe for Supplies to craft more powerful Outpost components, as well as gear for your operatives to face even tougher challengers.
If your frontline collapses, the enemy's Drop Pods will land closer toward the Outpost.
Artillery pieces are powerful weapons with incredibly long ranges. They can be fired manually or used during Command Mode.
Danger! You're gravely wounded! Please replenish your HP and Armor immediately.
The core of all Aethereum Weaponry. Can be infused with XEN to create weapons with unique performance.
Friendly miners can collect resources and provide a continuous stream of supplies.
Double Tap Barrel will allow you to shoot two rounds simultaneously for only one round at the cost of some damage.

Walls and pillars are only considered load-bearing when they connect vertically and directly to the A.T.C.. A component is considered unstable if it's not connected to a load-bearing structure within 3 component spaces.
Produces various models of the Guardian. Different types of Guardians can be equipped with different weapons.
Can be used to store ammo and automatically replenish ammo for nearby Assault Components. The resupply range is limited, so place them accordingly.
Power Banks serve multiple purposes. They can be used to either activate certain devices, recharge the Outpost's generator, or detonate to inflict substantial damage.

You can use [Trait Reforger] to gain Reforge Pts.
Use these points on the Trait menu to replae your active skills.

Earn Trait Pts through leveling up your Operative or using [Trait Enhancers].
Apply these points in the Trait interface to acquire new Traits.
Actions your character perform may cost Stamina. Running out of Stamina will put you in danger.
Set priorities on the turrets to make the optimal use of them.
Hitting an enemy's weak point will deal extra damage.
The Healing Station inside the Outpost will heal you.
Your Outpost requires Power to move to a new area. Without enough power, you'll be sent back to base.
The shop's items will refresh from time to time. They can be purchased with [Gold].
Drinking water will lead to a higher <DesTipShow_Bold>max</> Stamina and increased <DesTipShow_Bold>recovery speed</>. This effect can stack.
Recovery operations will alert enemies in the vicinity and potentially draw in enemy reinforcements.
A boss will appear in the last phase of Recovery Day, indicated by a skull icon on the progress bar in the top right corner of the UI.
The last phase of Recovery Day will spawn a gigantic enemy boss.
The recovery fleet will attract a large amount of hostile attention. Should the Core Tower be destroyed, AETOS will obliterate the recovery fleet, and all your collected items will be lost.
Containers can send items you recover to your [Field Storage].
Item glow can be adjusted within the settings menu. This will affect how easy it is to spot items while scavenging.
You have different Main Objectives in different areas: certain areas involve combat, while others are only for exploration.

Difficulty can be configured before starting an exploration. Increased difficulty leads to better rewards at a cost of greater risk. Clearing an area at a certain difficulty may unlock additional difficulty levels.
In Single Player, press Space to enable slow motion.
Explore each area and scavenge useful items. Place these items inside your backpack or a container to bring them back with you.
Repeatedly imbuing XEN into the same slot increases its EXP. Once the EXP bar fills, the XEN levels up, receiving an increased bonus and reduced drawback.
While in command mode, you can select the Outpost's components to activate their effects. Begin by manufacturing ammunition and activating the Outpost's turrets.
Holstering your weapon increases Stamina Recovery. Press [5] to holster your weapon.
Past tutorials are available in the Compendium on the pause menu.

During the preparation time in between each wave, you can move your Outpost to a new base.
You can do that while in Command Mode.
In the Paint Mode, the outpost's appearance can be customized. More paint schemes can be unlocked through game progression and exploration.
In edit mode, you can select multiple components by [dragging the mouse] or by holding [Shift + LMB].
Roguelike Mode offers a unique Trait selection mechanism, allowing for the creation of more formidable Operatives.
Staying in the area for longer means more dangerous enemies but also better loot.
Adjustment mode allows you to adjust the components on your Outpost, such as the turret's range. However, you'll not be able to move or install any new components.
After selecting the components, you can pick up all the components by clicking on one of them, and then uniformly adjust them, including moving, rotating, or deleting them.
Eliminate all enemy forces within the allotted time to claim the rewards. Rewards scale based on the time remaining.
Every site varied in risks and opportunities.
The interactive items on each map can be interacted with by pressing the [E] button.
Tanks shoot far and boast heavy armor. You'll need a larger caliber weapon to effectively deal with them.
Standing outside of the Promethean Field will lead to a quick and gruesome death from AETOS.
Most facilities must consume Power to function properly.
When a fog appears, your visibility and turret firing ranges will be reduced.
Losing your Armor makes you significantly more vulnerable. It's crucial to regularly replenish your Armor or use food to heal yourself.
An evac transport will be deployed immediately upon the destruction of a Core Tower. Make sure you get to it before the timer expires.
If you don't feel like exploring, you can use [Instant Loot] and immediately obtain part of the loot.
Selecting a "Risk Incident" will lead to higher risks during battle, but it will also lead to increased rewards.
What if I stick this on...?
If you've selected a movable unit, click RMB for them to move.
Completing the challenge will yield great rewards, some even include unique Outpost Components.
Optimal placement and settings of different turrets are crucial to leverage their individual advantages.
Find useful military assets to deploy for defense!
Store Mission Items in the containers of the Core Tower to recover them.
Beware of explosions. All the time. Because you and your squadmates' explosives can also knock you down. And you might lose a random item in your backpack if you get downed.
Select your units with LMB and give orders in the menu.
Support Facilities and Support Units on the carrier (inside the base) will be carried over to the next base.

A structure must be set on a load-bearing component connected vertically to the foundation (walls or pillars) to remain stable. If a load-bearing component is destroyed, all structures above it will crumble.

A structure must be set on a load-bearing component connected vertically to the foundation (walls or pillars) to remain stable. If a load-bearing component is destroyed, all structures above it will crumble.
When you find powerful ammo that is enhanced with elements like fire, ice or energy, hold [R] to bring up the wheel, and use [Mouse] to select ammo.
Stronger enemies should be prioritized first. Make sure to properly set each turret's targeting priority.
You can craft ammo when you've got plenty of resources.
Bosses may notice you after you finish exploring a Landmark. They will appear at some point in the final stage of the Recovery Day.
When your Outpost has many components, right-click on a component to lock it. This prevents accidental selection but allows you to place new components of the same type.
When your Outpost loses power, you'll lose access to all of its functions, making you incredibly vulnerable.
Activating the see-through mode will hide components above the set plane to simplify placing parts beneath it. The height of the see-through plane can be adjusted.
Watch out for Envoy attacks from behind!
Watch out for Nebulas dropping in from above!
Be careful! The operatives are extremely vulnerable without armor!
When you need to craft a component, you can jump to the crafting menu straight from the Outpost edit interface.
The bonus effect when a component is placed on the Outpost. This effect will become inactive if there is not enough power to supply the component.
You must first place the item you wish to recover inside the container before beginning Recovery operations.
Affects the damage dealt by components, the size of containers, the area of the X-Boost Field, and the defense bonus of your operative within range.
You have successfully completed an Exploration. Any scavenged items will be placed in field storage. They may not be used until they have been shipped back to base.

Congratulations on completing the challenge! You can choose to evacuate and call a transport ship to return to the base,
or you can choose to continue playing like in Endless Mode.
Note: As you continue to defend, the strength of the enemies will increase with each wave, so be mindful and choose an appropriate time to retreat.

You can also deploy Support Facilities to strengthen your perimeter. Support Facilities can only be built inside a X-Boost Field.
Now, let's build an Auto Turret.
As you progress, you'll unlock new ways to play, including on-the-fly switching to command mode to command the Outpost and its support units, piloting Cataphracts, or engaging personally in combat.
Use photo mode to capture screenshots. This mode allows for the adjustment of filters and hiding of the interface, without altering the outpost's settings.
In command mode, you have the ability to deploy various Outpost Components. Now, try launching attacks using this Prewar Artillery against the enemy.
You can import an Outpost share code you received, or export your design to be shared with others.

You can relocate your Outpost to a nearby base.
Different bases have different enemy attack routes, as well as different rewards and challenges.
You can save changes made to your Outpost by pressing [Ctrl + S]. If you did not save, you'll be asked to either save or discard any changes you have made when you exit the mode.

You can assign control groups by pressing [Ctrl + Num Key]. Use [Shift + Num Key] to change the control group number, and press the [Num Key] to select the corresponding control group. Control groups assigned in Adjustment Mode will be carried over into battle.
You can press [F] to toggle See-Through Mode. Under this mode, components above a specified height will be made invisible and unselectable. You can adjust this height by using [Shift + LMB].
You can drag the cursor or hold [Shift + LMB] to select multiple components.

You can send materials back to the Base Warehouse by recovering drones/containers or the Oribital Strike Cannon.
Some materials in your character's backpack will also be returned to the base warehouse after a successful retreat.
You can create a buffer zone by building the Core as it will force the enemies to drop further away from you. In addition, it will activate nearby facilities, acting as a temporary defense line.
You can now relocate your base while in Command Mode.

You can alter the difficulty settings in this mode.
The difficulty level affects the overall challenge of gameplay and also determines the quality and rarity of the items dropped.
You can press [M] at any time to enter Command Mode to manage or command your Outpost.
You have found a Core Gun. You can swap it out by either dragging or double-clicking in your backpack. Each Core Gun features its own unique effects and XEN recipes.
Uninsured gear will be lost if go missing or die in the battle.

You must limit your activities within the confines of the Promethean Field. Should you venture out too far, you'll be crushed to a paste by AETOS.
The Promethean Field will shrink during battle, with its range expanding as you complete more waves.

The Intelligence Center will try to establish contact with civilian militias, offering you the chance to accompany them on Tours. Successfully completing the mission will lead to their incorporation into the EUA.
You can unlock Underbarrel Auto Turret in the Research Center after taking it back to the base.

Two gameplay modes are available:
[Outpost Mode], the main game mode,
and [Return to Dawn Base], introduced in later updates for faster-paced combat and exploration.
The latter is ideal for players who want to jump into the action, as well as those looking to test their Outpost's power.
We still recommend trying [Outpost Mode] first to experience the unique gameplay mechanics.
The longer the tour route, the more enemies we might have to face at the end
The Guardian's jump ability lets you move easily across difficult terrain and can be used to evade incoming damage.
Shields greatly reduce the amount of damage taken in battle and regenerate quickly in the X-Boost Field. They can also provide extra bonuses when modified with gear.
MG Turrets struggle to destroy armored enemy tanks, which are better countered by Light Guns.
Press to toggle your flashlight.
Press or to unleash Fae's skills. Charged Fire: Gain unlimited ammo for a brief period. Instant Cooldown: Greatly reduces the cooldown of the equipped XEN.
Press [2] to switch to the Slingshot XEN.
Press [2] to switch to the Slingshot XEN. Aim it at the ground to deploy it.
Press to enter Command Mode.
Press to move.
Press [5] to draw or holster your weapon.
Press [B] for backpack. Hold [LMB] to drag items, [R] to rotate, [RMB] for details.
Press [G] to use the selected item. Now, use this ammo crate.
Press [M] to enter Command Mode, where you can deploy support facilities, use their abilities, and control your units.
Press [Space] to jump. Jump near objects in order to climb.
Press [V] to perform a melee attack.
Click [LMB] to shoot, and click [RMB] to toggle aiming down your sight. Press [R] to reload your weapon.
Press [C] to crouch; press and hold [C] to slide while Sprinting.
[Hold Q] Injecting Aether into your weapon instantly reloads your magazine and infuse all rounds with the specified elemental property.
Holding [Shift] will allow you to use the skill of all selected Components all at once.
Press and hold [Tab] to toggle Quick Slot.

While holding [Tab], use your mouse to select an item and release [Tab] to place it in the Quick Slot.
In FPS Mode, press [G] to immediately use the item in the Quick Slot.
Hold [RMB] to shoot with the handheld muzzle.
Exploration lasts several rounds (days). Items obtained through Exploration must be retrieved by a recovery fleet at the end of the last day.
Gather resources, construct defenses, and survive as long as possible or bring back more loot.
Hangar that houses one Cataphract. While on tour, you may pilot the Cataphract yourself, or launch it autonomously.
Camera movement can be toggled between [Free Mode] and [Horizontal Mode] by pressing the [R] button.
Destroying the enemy's signal tower will stop this current attack wave. Focus on locating and demolishing it with heavy firepower.
Attack the Kronos' weak points to deal higher damage, and break them to receive advanced component reagents.
Attack the Crius' weak points to deal higher damage, and break them to receive advanced component reagents.
Place it inside the Small Generator to produce Power.
Enemy Armor will take reduced Kinetic Damage but takes increased Pyro Damage.
Enemy types/stats and the direction of their attack will increase as you clear more waves.
Enemy Drop Pods can be destroyed by heavy firepower.

This means that the components used on the Outpost exceeds the current amount stored in your storage. When this happens, you'll be unable to save your changes unless you remove the insufficient component.
Drones will carry resources back to the base no matter if the mission is successful or not. The resources will be carried back to the [user's] base.
In Endless Mode, each Core provides extra items and event rewards and expands the Promethean Field.
Keep note of the [Intel] section in the top right corner.
Nebulas are dangerous creatures that prioritize attacking your operatives.
Rainstorms may cause lightning to strike the area. Steer clear.
When a blizzard strikes, you'll receive reduced movement speed when outdoors, slower stamina regeneration, increased susceptibility to Cryo Damage, and a higher likelihood of being frozen.
Pick out a sight that fits your play style.
There's plenty of time to set up a proper defense. Three additional defensive perimeters are required to repel the enemy's attack.
Aim at the ground to deploy the slingshot.
Shoot at explosives to detonate them
This area is under enemy surveillance. They will start attacking us once we start recovery.
This area is free for exploration. We can head back at any time.
This area is rather safe. We won't be attacked during recovery.

No mission in the area. You are free to explore the area and leave through the Core Tower whenever you feel like.
Weapon Attachments greatly enhance certain performance parameters of a weapon. Adapt different attachments towards different battlefield scenarios.
Your progress will be saved at the end of each Exploration Day.
There's at least one Landmark in each area, where you'll encounter enemies even more ferocious; you may also find better treasures here.
Each challenge mode features unique opportunities and challenges, detailed in the event list.
Each Specialization comes with two active skills and multiple passive skills, which can turn the tide at critical moments.
Each operative can possess a multitude of skills, including Specialization, Traits, and Gear skills.

Each formation has a center core that must be protected. If destroyed, the Promethean Field will shrink in size and the enemy's offensive will ramp up in intensity. Once all formations have been lost, all operatives will make a last stand at the Outpost and await extraction.
Each merchant has their specific preferences. They let you purchase or trade in items. They make periodic appearances at the Trade Port.
When you finish a site, you might receive an "event". Choose with caution.
Each enemy attack may bring with it random Hazards. Carefully strategize your defenses and supplies in advance to handle these events.

Every few waves cleared will cause the next wave to become an [Elite Wave]. Keep your eye out for the mission info on the top right of the screen. The enemies will attack more fiercely as well as spawn more [Apostles] and [Seraphs].
They're stronger, more dangerous, and more visible than their peers. Prioritize neutralizing them first.
During a sandstorm, the strong electromagnetic interference will prevent you from using Command Mode.
You've run out of ammo boxes! Craft some in the Ammo Producer.
You've run out of ammo! Craft some in the Ammo Producer.
Useful intel can be seen on each site's info panel.
Dedicate personnel to a certain department for increased [Efficiency]. As the number increases, you will unlock a greater [Efficiency Level] and gain access to certain buffs.
XEN changes how bullets behave. Hold [Q] to open Core Gun.
Use [scroll wheel] to select a XEN and click [LMB] to infuse it into the Core Gun.
Pyro ammo is effective against armored enemies.

Click the button here to choose among various Challenges. Each Challenge comes with its own set of events and encounters.
Use these Challenges to familiarize yourself with the game and its many mechanics.
Explosive Bolts will explode briefly after hitting a target. Effective against enemies in a group.
Explosives are great for clearing out obstacles or discovering items hiding within the scenery.
Resources are full. Use them to craft ammo boxes.
Specially marked walls can be destroyed by explosives.
Each Operative possesses unique traits that can be activated using Trait Pts; these points are acquired as they level up.
Now click on the [Ammo Crate] to select it.
Used to craft units and supplies during battle, as well as deploy Support Facilities.
Cabin where you can manually control all nearby turrets, allowing you to concentrate your firepower or cease firing.

Used to power the Outpost and its various systems, as well as aid in exploration. Keep an eye out for your Power—if it drops below zero, Core Output will be significantly lowered, compromising the performance of your Outpost.
Negative Power: When Power drops below zero, the Core Output will be significantly lowered, leaving your squad in jeopardy!
Power is a valuable and limited resource. Some backdrop props or Outpost Components will allow you to replenish them.
Keep an eye out for items that can replenish your Power and resources!
Once complete, you'll be able to produce [30mm Light Gun]! They'll help you against armored enemy targets.
Research Center: Spend your Research Points to unlock new technologies. Progress in research and development increases as your battle with the enemy continues.
The Crius is swift as a shadow. Seize your chance and attack as it dives!

Free Mode for Return to Dawn Base has now been unlocked. You can adjust the difficulty and game rules based on the strength of your Outpost or your Operative to tailor the most suitable game experience for yourself.
A Core provides a large amount of Supply and Power generation. Enemies killed inside a Core's X-Boost Field will be converted into Power.
Nearby POIs will be marked out on your map. That's where all the loot is at.

Hazards significantly increase the game's difficulty,
whereas Fortunes boost the combat abilities of the Outpost or your Operative.

Hazards can bring astonishing benefits as well as formidable challenges, so choose carefully the Hazards you wish to include. You can remove or add Hazards using Power on the preparation page on Recovery Day.
